title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. trachycarpum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. trachycarpum, also known as wild angelica, is a subspecies of the angelica plant native to Europe and Western Asia. This biennial plant is prized for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of wild angelica include relieving respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. Wild angelica is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
